perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, "Proprietary/Confidential Information" shall mean any and all proprietary, secret information, technical data or know-how related to any aspect of other party's business or technology including, without limitation, data, know- how, formulas, designs, photographs, drawings, specification, software programs and samples and any other material bearing or incorporating any such information which is disclosed by one party to the other, which information, data or know-how is marked or stipulated as being "Proprietary", "Confidential", "Strictly Private" or otherwise, using words of similar significance.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, "Proprietary/Confidential Information" shall mean any and all proprietary, secret information, technical data or know-how related to any aspect of other party's business or technology including, without limitation, data, know- how, formulas, designs, photographs, drawings, specification, software programs and samples and any other material bearing or incorporating any such information which is disclosed by one party to the other, which information, data or know-how is marked or stipulated as being "Proprietary", "Private" or otherwise, using words of similar significance.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'Confidential' and 'Strictly Private' from the definition of Proprietary/Confidential Information creates a contradiction. Later clauses refer to 'Proprietary/Confidential Information' but now the definition excludes information marked 'Confidential' or 'Strictly Private', leading to confusion on what is covered. This change directly creates a contradiction with section 2, where the receiving party undertakes to maintain all Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties in confidence, because now the definition is ambigious.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereby undertakes to treat and maintain all Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties in confidence. With respect thereto, each party hereby undertakes and agrees as follows: <PAGE> i. For a period of 5 years from the date of this Agreement, the receiving party shall not publish, disseminate nor disclose any Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties to any third party accept to those of its own employment having valid need to know the information in the course of employment and such disclosure shall be on terms not less restrictive than those herein contained. ii. The receiving party shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Proprietary/Confidential Information as it uses in respect of its own information of like importance but in no case less than a reasonable degree of care. iii. The receiving party shall in accordance with the request of the other parties, either return all copies, recording and tangible manifestations of Proprietary/Confidential Information or destroy the same following a determination by any of the parties not to enter into any arrangement or venture with each other of the kind contemplated herein or upon termination of any related memorandum of understanding or agreement entered into between the parties or upon the written request of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'Confidential' and 'Strictly Private' from the definition of 'Proprietary/Confidential Information'. The contradicted text requires all 'Proprietary/Confidential Information' to be kept confidential. Since the changed text no longer includes information marked 'Confidential' or 'Strictly Private' within the definition of 'Proprietary/Confidential Information', a clear contradiction is created because the receiving party is now not explicitly obligated to maintain the confidentiality of information marked as 'Confidential' or 'Strictly Private', even though the contradicted text states they should maintain all 'Proprietary/Confidential Information' in confidence.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of 5 years from the date of this Agreement, the receiving party shall not publish, disseminate nor disclose any Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties to any third party accept to those of its own employment having valid need to know the information in the course of employment and such disclosure shall be on terms not less restrictive than those herein contained.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of 5 years from the date of this Agreement, the receiving party shall not publish, disseminate nor disclose any Proprietary Information received from any of the other parties to any third party accept to those of its own employment having valid need to know the information in the course of employment and such disclosure shall be on terms not less restrictive than those herein contained.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Proprietary/Confidential Information' to just 'Proprietary Information' in this clause, we create a contradiction. The agreement is titled 'MUTUAL CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT' and other sections refer to handling 'Proprietary/Confidential Information'. This change suggests that some confidential information may be excluded from the non-disclosure obligations, this change directly creates a contradiction to section 9 'REMEDY FOR BREACH', because now is unclear if confidential information is protected for this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is understood and agreed between the parties that any breach of the obligations of confidentiality contained in this Agreement may cause the disclosing party irreparable loss. Accordingly, and in addition to any other remedies a party may have in law or equity, the disclosing party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the receiving party to prevent any further or continuing breach of the receiving party's obligations or additional damage to the disclosing party in the event such loss is in fact incurred by the disclosing party as a result of the breach or is imminent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Proprietary/Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information'. The original text and the contradicted text explicitly mention 'confidentiality' and obligations surrounding 'confidential information'. This change creates an inconsistency as it is now unclear whether 'confidential information' is protected under section 9 'REMEDY FOR BREACH', causing a strong contradiction by creating ambiguity where certainty existed before.

perturbation.original_text: The aforesaid restrictions on the parties shall not apply to any Proprietary/Confidential Information which i. Can be proved by documentary evidence to be such Proprietary/Confidential Information that was already in the possession of the receiving party and at its free disposal before the disclosure hereunder to it; ii. Is received by the receiving party from third parties without accompanying secrecy or confidentiality obligations and not in violation of any duty of confidence under this agreement; iii. Is independently developed by the receiving party; iv. Is or becomes generally available to the public in printed publications in general discussion through no act or default on the part of the receiving party or its agents or employees; v. Is furnished to a third party by a party hereunto who owns such Proprietary/Confidential Information without similar restriction on the third party's rights; vi. Is approved for release by written authorization of the other party; or vii. Is disclosed pursuant to any requirement or request by operation of law provided that the involving party shall prior to disclosure notify the disclosing party of any such requirement or request.
perturbation.changed_text: The aforesaid restrictions on the parties shall not apply to any Proprietary Information which i. Can be proved by documentary evidence to be such Proprietary Information that was already in the possession of the receiving party and at its free disposal before the disclosure hereunder to it; ii. Is received by the receiving party from third parties without accompanying secrecy or confidentiality obligations and not in violation of any duty of confidence under this agreement; iii. Is independently developed by the receiving party; iv. Is or becomes generally available to the public in printed publications in general discussion through no act or default on the part of the receiving party or its agents or employees; v. Is furnished to a third party by a party hereunto who owns such Proprietary Information without similar restriction on the third party's rights; vi. Is approved for release by written authorization of the other party; or vii. Is disclosed pursuant to any requirement or request by operation of law provided that the involving party shall prior to disclosure notify the disclosing party of any such requirement or request.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Proprietary/Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information' creates inconsistency with the agreement's overall intent to protect both proprietary and confidential data. If proprietary information is excluded, this creates a loophole where a party could argue that certain confidential information is not subject to the restrictions, this is contradicting to the title of the agreement and section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereby undertakes to treat and maintain all Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties in confidence. With respect thereto, each party hereby undertakes and agrees as follows: <PAGE> i. For a period of 5 years from the date of this Agreement, the receiving party shall not publish, disseminate nor disclose any Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties to any third party accept to those of its own employment having valid need to know the information in the course of employment and such disclosure shall be on terms not less restrictive than those herein contained. ii. The receiving party shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Proprietary/Confidential Information as it uses in respect of its own information of like importance but in no case less than a reasonable degree of care. iii. The receiving party shall in accordance with the request of the other parties, either return all copies, recording and tangible manifestations of Proprietary/Confidential Information or destroy the same following a determination by any of the parties not to enter into any arrangement or venture with each other of the kind contemplated herein or upon termination of any related memorandum of understanding or agreement entered into between the parties or upon the written request of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Proprietary/Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information' directly contradicts the contradicted text, which repeatedly refers to and protects 'Proprietary/Confidential Information'. By removing 'Confidential Information' from the exception in the changed text, it creates a loophole where confidential information might not be subject to the same restrictions, thus contradicting the intent and explicit language of the contradicted section, which aims to protect both. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the contract uses different terms (Proprietary vs. Proprietary/Confidential) leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All Proprietary/Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing party. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or confirming any rights by license or otherwise expressly impliedly or otherwise, for any of the Proprietary/Confidential Information disclosed by the disclosing party hereunder. All Proprietary/Confidential Information, existing in written form or recorded in any other tangible medium, shall be returned to the disclosing party upon its request, together with any reproductions or copies thereof. Further, upon the disclosing party's request, notes, memoranda and reports which incorporate the Proprietary/Confidential Information shall, without exception, be destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing party. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or confirming any rights by license or otherwise expressly impliedly or otherwise, for any of the Confidential Information disclosed by the disclosing party hereunder. All Confidential Information, existing in written form or recorded in any other tangible medium, shall be returned to the disclosing party upon its request, together with any reproductions or copies thereof. Further, upon the disclosing party's request, notes, memoranda and reports which incorporate the Confidential Information shall, without exception, be destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Proprietary/Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Information' makes proprietary data vulnerable to misuse and challenges the entire framework for data protection established at the beginning of the contract. This is contradicting to the intent of the first section 'INTERPRETATION'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, "Proprietary/Confidential Information" shall mean any and all proprietary, secret information, technical data or know-how related to any aspect of other party's business or technology including, without limitation, data, know- how, formulas, designs, photographs, drawings, specification, software programs and samples and any other material bearing or incorporating any such information which is disclosed by one party to the other, which information, data or know-how is marked or stipulated as being "Proprietary", "Confidential", "Strictly Private" or otherwise, using words of similar significance. Such disclosure may be made either directly or indirectly, in writing, orally or by drawings, plans or inspection of products, materials, parts or equipment.
perturbation.justification: The original text protects both 'Proprietary/Confidential Information,' while the changed text only protects 'Confidential Information.' The 'Contradicted Text' section defines 'Proprietary/Confidential Information' as a single encompassing term. By removing 'Proprietary' from the changed text, it creates a scenario where proprietary information, as defined in the 'Contradicted Text,' is no longer explicitly protected under the clauses regarding return and destruction of information, thus leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event the disclosing party of such Proprietary/Confidential Information orally discloses the information to the receiving party, the disclosing party agrees to promptly notify the receiving party of the confidentiality of such oral disclosure and reduces to writing such Proprietary/Confidential Information and submit the same to the receiving party within 15 days of such oral disclosure, upon which the receiving party shall not be bound by the confidentiality obligations as herein provided as regards the said Proprietary/Confidential Information disclosed orally.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event the disclosing party of such Proprietary Information orally discloses the information to the receiving party, the disclosing party agrees to promptly notify the receiving party of the confidentiality of such oral disclosure and reduces to writing such Proprietary Information and submit the same to the receiving party within 15 days of such oral disclosure, upon which the receiving party shall not be bound by the confidentiality obligations as herein provided as regards the said Proprietary Information disclosed orally.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Proprietary/Confidential Information' to just 'Proprietary Information' in the context of oral disclosure, it implies that orally disclosed confidential information isn't protected if not put in writing. This undermines the confidentiality aspect and contradicts section 2's broad undertaking to maintain all Proprietary/Confidential Information received in confidence. The main intent of this clause is for Proprietary/Confidential Information to be protected, but by changing to 'Proprietary Information' isn't being protected anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereby undertakes to treat and maintain all Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties in confidence. With respect thereto, each party hereby undertakes and agrees as follows: <PAGE> i. For a period of 5 years from the date of this Agreement, the receiving party shall not publish, disseminate nor disclose any Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties to any third party accept to those of its own employment having valid need to know the information in the course of employment and such disclosure shall be on terms not less restrictive than those herein contained. ii. The receiving party shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Proprietary/Confidential Information as it uses in respect of its own information of like importance but in no case less than a reasonable degree of care. iii. The receiving party shall in accordance with the request of the other parties, either return all copies, recording and tangible manifestations of Proprietary/Confidential Information or destroy the same following a determination by any of the parties not to enter into any arrangement or venture with each other of the kind contemplated herein or upon termination of any related memorandum of understanding or agreement entered into between the parties or upon the written request of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Proprietary/Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information' in the context of oral disclosure creates a contradiction with the contradicted text. The contradicted text states that 'Each party hereby undertakes to treat and maintain all Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties in confidence.' By limiting the protection of orally disclosed information to only 'Proprietary Information,' the changed text implies that orally disclosed confidential information is not protected unless written. This directly conflicts with the broad confidentiality undertaking in the contradicted text, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that necessary authorizations, permits or licenses including expert licenses as may be required will be obtained prior to the exportation/disclosure of any Proprietary/Confidential Information relating to the technology of the other party. The disclosing party shall notify the receiving party of the need to obtain any required authorizations, permits and licenses and/or the need to comply with any relevant laws or regulations relating to the disclosure. The disclosing party shall obtain the required authorizations, permits and licenses.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that necessary authorizations, permits or licenses including expert licenses as may be required will be obtained prior to the exportation/disclosure of any Proprietary Information relating to the technology of the other party. The disclosing party shall notify the receiving party of the need to obtain any required authorizations, permits and licenses and/or the need to comply with any relevant laws or regulations relating to the disclosure. The disclosing party shall obtain the required authorizations, permits and licenses.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Proprietary/Confidential Information' to just 'Proprietary Information' in this clause, the agreement weakens control over confidential data transfers. The exporting parties might perceive that confidential information lacks stringent export/disclosure control measures, causing discrepancies and is creating an In-Text contradiction between the title of the document 'MUTUAL CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MUTUAL CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This Agreement is made and entered into on the 10th day of September, 1997. BETWEEN: (1) Creative Labs, Inc. having the principal offices at 1901 McCarthy Boulevard, Milpitas, CA 95085 ("Creative"). (2) Pacific Magtron, Inc. a California corporation having its principal offices at 1800 California Circle, Milpitas, CA 95085 (" ") WHEREAS: 1. Creative and Pacific Magtron are engaged in discussions with respect to a possible business or financial arrangement or venture between them relating to multimedia technology. 2. In connection therewith disclosure of certain information which is proprietary/confidential to the parties may become necessary or desirable. 3. Each party is willing to disclose such Proprietary/Confidential Information to the other parties upon the terms and conditions herein set forth and each party is willing to maintain the confidentiality of such information disclosed to it by the other parties in accordance with the terms and conditions hereof. NOW THEREFORE, in consideration of the disclosure of such Proprietary/Confidential Information and the mutual covenants and promises herein contained, it is agreed as follows: 1. INTERPRETATION For the purposes of this Agreement, "Proprietary/Confidential Information" shall mean any and all proprietary, secret information, technical data or know-how related to any aspect of other party's business or technology including, without limitation, data, know- how, formulas, designs, photographs, drawings, specification, software programs and samples and any other material bearing or incorporating any such information which is disclosed by one party to the other, which information, data or know-how is marked or stipulated as being "Proprietary", "Confidential", "Strictly Private" or otherwise, using words of similar significance. Such disclosure may be made either directly or indirectly, in writing, orally or by drawings, plans or inspection of products, materials, parts or equipment. 2. UNDERTAKING OF THE PARTIES Each party hereby undertakes to treat and maintain all Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties in confidence. With respect thereto, each party hereby undertakes and agrees as follows: <PAGE> i. For a period of 5 years from the date of this Agreement, the receiving party shall not publish, disseminate nor disclose any Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties to any third party accept to those of its own employment having valid need to know the information in the course of employment and such disclosure shall be on terms not less restrictive than those herein contained. ii. The receiving party shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Proprietary/Confidential Information as it uses in respect of its own information of like importance but in no case less than a reasonable degree of care. iii. The receiving party shall in accordance with the request of the other parties, either return all copies, recording and tangible manifestations of Proprietary/Confidential Information or destroy the same following a determination by any of the parties not to enter into any arrangement or venture with each other of the kind contemplated herein or upon termination of any related memorandum of understanding or agreement entered into between the parties or upon the written request of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Proprietary/Confidential Information,' while the changed text only mentions 'Proprietary Information.' This creates a contradiction with the title and purpose of the agreement, 'MUTUAL CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT', and specifically contradicts clause 1 which defines the agreement as pertaining to 'Proprietary/Confidential Information'. By removing the confidential aspect in the changed text, the altered clause weakens control over confidential data transfers and conflicts with the explicitly stated purpose and scope of the original agreement, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
